behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the segmentation logic itself: given one command string with newlines, it must emit multiple segments. No filesystem, subprocess, or network is required to expose this bug.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmentation function with a multiline shell block (assignments + command) and assert it returns distinct ordered segments for each newline statement, not a single combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Real failure came from interaction between segmentation and trigger classification. Testing classifier alone could miss wiring/normalization issues across modules.
behaviors.test_description: Run the PostToolUse classification path end-to-end in-process (event payload -> segmentation -> trigger classifier) with a multiline block containing assignments then `gh pr create`; assert final classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is the negative counterpart of behavior 2 and depends on the same segmentation+classification wiring; isolated mocks can hide misrouting/regression in combined logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run the same PostToolUse classification pipeline with multiline assignments ending in `gh issue create`; assert result is non-PR classification and specifically not PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: This crosses parser/identifier reconstruction and state persistence (filesystem). A pure unit test of reconstruction or serialization alone can miss real write-path bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute PostToolUse handler with a PR-create trigger fixture and verify it derives the expected PR identifier and writes it to the review-loop state file in the configured local path.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary includes cross-process contract between two hook entrypoints via OS filesystem state. In-process mocks may pass while actual hook subprocess invocation or file-handling semantics fail.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke PostToolUse hook entrypoint to write gate state, then invoke PreToolUse hook entrypoint as a separate process against that state and assert enforcement decision matches expected gated behavior.
